entry.title: Re: BAPI_PO_CREATE1: Error Message ME083 - Enter GL/Account
entry.content: The error ME083 occurs because SAP has a 99-line limit for multiple account assignments in BAPI_PO_CREATE1. To resolve this, you need to split the PO item into multiple items once the number of service lines exceeds 99. Each new item should have the same material/service details and continue assigning the remaining service lines, ensuring that no single item exceeds the 99-line restriction.

entry.title: Re: Product compliance events and integration
entry.content:
Hello krishg,

currently it is not possible to use the Product Compliance Events and Tasks for your scenario, as they cannot be sent to an event mesh. 

The way to access the product compliance relevant data is by using the public APIs. Here a list of all available APIs: https://api.sap.com/search?searchterm=SAP_COM_0797&tab=APIs&$refinedBy=true&$type=(%22API%22,%22API%20Package%22).

However, this only allows a pull of the data from third party systems and no active push from the SAP system. 

If there are APIs missing or you definitely need a push mechanism from the SAP system, you can add an improvement request to the Continuous Influence Portal for Product Compliance: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3334

Hopefully this helps.

Best regards,

Markus

entry.title: FWZE Amount not correct
entry.content:
I use FWZZ --> FWZE 

Units = 9000 +400 -100-8000= 1300

Why amount caculate = 9.400 * 400 = 3.760.000

How to caculate amount = 400 * 1300 = 520.000

entry.title: Re: BR1585E BR*Tools parameter 'active_pdbs' is set for 'NON_MULTI_TENANT_DB'
entry.content: Make sure the "actve_pdbs" parameter for BR*Tools is set only in case of available pluggable databases. It can be set only for Multitenant databases. In case the target system is not yet configured as a Multitenant, then it must not be set.

entry.title: Re: How do we call SOLR externally with SSO enabled?
entry.content:
Disabling SSO for Solr in Commerce Cloud is against security regulations.
"3320534 - How to connect Solr through cURL/Postman in SAP Commerce" https://me.sap.com/notes/3320534/E
Solr Security https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/b2f400d4c0414461a4bb7e115dccd779/f7251d5a1d6848489b1ce7ba46300fe6.html#solr-security

When SSO would be disabled, SAP security measures are no longer upheld and SAP cannot monitor environment in terms of secureness as effectively. Solr SSO was introduced to protect Solr endpoints even if they were set to public, intentionally or not. When one disables SSO the Solr admin console is not protected and anybody can login.

Disabling SSO for Solr in general means:
- Reduced security to access Solr
- No guarantee in reference to security implications around Solr

To access Solr API with SSO enabled one could make use of the OCC API for data exchange.

One could extend the Commerce OCC API, which would gather the relevant data from Solr. Utilizing this method, one would need though to ensure on customer's side that the filtering settings on the endpoint where OCC API is set up, are strict enough to guarantee security. Furthermore, such a configuration has the added benefit of the authentication mechanisms of OCC.

Please check documentation on OCC implementation:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/3476714bba0b4cb9b3eb58c270e44439/8c19ab00866910148f87bf32d4a60d38.html
https://help.sap.com/doc/ab7bf5127abb4c67a55df4ea67507511/2211/en-US/index.html#_overview

entry.title: How to: Extension of WebDynpro Configurations due to changes in CDS Views
entry.content:
The backend and WebDynPro must be properly configured to enable a field for filtering in a WebDynpro application. This involves modifying the CDS (Core Data Services) views to ensure the field is recognised and available for filtering in the frontend configuration.

This topic concerns these Fiori Apps, but the principle is the same for each WebDynpro App.

CDS Query (object)CDS Query (2C+SQL View)CDS Query WebDynpro ConfigurationApp ID
C_CostCenterQ2001CFICOSTCTRQ2001FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTCCF0963
C_CostCenterPlanActQ2001CFICCPLANACT2001FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTCC01W0081
C_ProfitCenterQ2701CFIPROFCTRQ2701FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTPCF0959
C_ProfitCenterPlanActQ2701CFIPCPLANACT2701FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTPC01W0079
C_MarketSegmentQ2501CFIMARKSEGMQ2501FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTMSF0960
C_MarketSegmentPlanActQ2501CFIMSPLANACT2501FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTMS01W0078
C_ProfitAndLossQ2901CFIPROFLOSSQ2901FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTPLF0958
C_ProfitAndLossPlanActQ2903CFIPLPLANACTFIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTPL03F1710
C_FunctionalAreasQ2801CFIFAQ2801FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTFAF1583A
C_FunctionalAreaPlanActQ2801CFIFAPLANACT2801FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTFA01W0077
C_InternalOrderPlanActQ2101CFIIOPLANACT2101FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTIO01W0076
C_SalesOrderQ2301CFISALESORDQ2301FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTSOF1582A
C_ProjectQ2201CFIPROJECTQ2201FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTPR F0961
C_ProjectPlanActQ2201CFIPRPLANACT2201FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTPR01W0080

Backend changes in CDS views:

Variant 1: Extending the CDS View

If the field is not yet available for filtering, you must modify the CDS view and add the appropriate annotations.

  •  Create a new CDS view extension and ensure you use your naming conventions where required.

 Important: Replace placeholders such as ZXXCOSTEXT,  C_CostCenterQ2001, ZXX_C_COST_CENTER_EXT, and I_JournalEntryItemCube.SourceLedger with names relevant to your implementation. These correspond to:

  • Catalog name
  • CDS view name
  • CDS extension view name
  • Field name

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXCOSTEXT'
extend view C_CostCenterQ2001 with ZXX_C_COST_CENTER_EXT
{
@Consumption.filter: { selectionType: #SINGLE, multipleSelections: true, mandatory: false }
I_JournalEntryItemCube.SourceLedger

}

 

  • Save and activate the CDS view

This will add Source Ledger to the C_CostCenterQ2001 query as a Filter. However, to see it within the Fiori App, you need to change the configuration.

Variant 2: Using Metadata Extension

If the field already exists in a standard CDS view but does not have a filter annotation, you can use a metadata extension instead of modifying the view directly:

  • Ensure Metadata Extensions Are Allowed (If not, you will need to use Variant 1.).
  • If you do not have this annotation present in the queries mentioned above, you will need to install this SAP Note 3509451 to your system.
  • Create a Metadata Extension

Important: Replace placeholders such as CostCenterQ2001 and CalendarWeek with names relevant to your implementation. These correspond to:

  • CDS view name
  • Field name

 

 

@Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER
annotate entity C_CostCenterQ2001
    with 
{
    @Consumption.filter: { selectionType: #SINGLE, multipleSelections: true, mandatory: false }
    CalendarWeek;
    
}

 

I would suggest you use @Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER, so it is determined what was done.

WebDynpro configuration:

  • Open the Fiori application Cost Centers – Actuals. Right-click in the filter area and select ‘Technical Help’.  Technical Help window will open.

  • In the technical help window, click on the shown area (Application Configuration).  A new window will open.

  • In the application configuration window, click on the Configuration Name.  You will be redirected to the Page Schema.

  • Scroll down to locate the Overview Page Schema. Expand the Page Header Area and then click on Config ID.

  • In the newly opened window, drag the desired filter into the Page Header Area within the schema below.

  • Click save.

Once all these steps are done you will see the field in the search filter among other fields like Ledger, Fiscal Year of Ledger etc., the position on where the field appears is up to you.

 

I would like to say a big thanks to my colleague, who helped me with creating this blog and preparing the documentation.

entry.title: Re: #COE#TAIT Service contract and service order billing...
entry.content:
Hello,

To find service contract and service order billing-related CDS views, as well as those related to billing plans and actual billing, you can utilize the View Browser app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Here’s how you can proceed:

1. **Access the View Browser App**: This application can be accessed within your SAP Fiori launchpad.

2. **Search for Relevant CDS Views**:
- Use keywords like "Service Contract Billing," "Service Order Billing," "Billing Plan," and "Actual Billing" to search for the relevant CDS views.
- Look for views that might have these terms in their titles or descriptions.

3. **Review Annotations**:
- Once you've identified potential CDS views, check their details under the Annotation tab specifically looking at `ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities`. This will help in understanding the specific use cases they are best suited for.

4. **Check for Predefined Standard CDS Views**:
- It’s also recommended to look for predefined standard CDS views that SAP provides. These are generally useful for reporting and analytics purposes. Here is a list of CDS views for Service 

CDS Views for Service

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: The Case for Upgrades in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - New whitepaper published
entry.content:
Is your business still running an older version of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition? You could be missing out on critical innovations and putting your system at risk. Friendly reminder: Mainstream and extended maintenance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems on versions 1709, 1809, 1909, and 2020 will conclude in December 2025. Now is the perfect time to plan your upgrade and stay ahead with the latest features and support!

 

Read the whitepaper, "Embrace agility and upgrade for success."

 

 Why Upgrade? It’s More Than Just a Task

Upgrading your SAP system isn't just about keeping up; it's about gaining a strategic advantage. Think of it as future-proofing your business. Here's what you stand to gain:

  • Supercharged Security: Outdated systems are vulnerable. Regular upgrades keep you protected with the latest cybersecurity measures and data protection. SAP only guarantees security updates for the most recent feature and support packages, so staying current is essential.
  • Innovation at Your Fingertips: Running on the latest release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition gives you access to continuous innovation, including advanced business AI, automation, and predictive analytics. New features like the AI copilot Joule, Microsoft Teams integration, and SAP Fiori improvements boost productivity and user experience. (see full list here). These innovations are included in your subscription—make sure you don’t miss out!
  • Cut Costs and Complexity: Sticking with older versions often leads to costly and complex custom code. Regular upgrades in combination with a clean core approach can help you stay closer to the SAP standard, making your system more agile and easier to manage.
  • Maximize Your ROI: You've already invested in SAP. By upgrading, you're maximizing that investment. You're unlocking the full potential of your system and getting the most value out of your license.
  • Practice makes Perfect: It's like training a muscle. The more often you upgrade, the easier the process becomes. Regular upgrades equip your team with the know-how for faster, smoother projects with fewer disruptions. Customers like Hitachi High-Tech have reduced their upgrade projects to just a single month by adopting a regular upgrade approach. Read Hitachi´s complete upgrade story.

Build your Upgrade Routine:

Establishing your governance strategy and plan to fit your individual operation and needs is key. SAP recommends that you run on the latest release or at least one below the latest, aligning your IT change management and business release strategy, as well as your project schedule, with SAP S/4HANA release timelines. As announced with the new SAP S/4HANA release and maintenance strategy, new releases are delivered every second year, with three feature and support packages in between, offering you greater flexibility and easier-to-consume innovation.

SAP is Here to Help You Every Step of the Way

Upgrading doesn't have to be daunting. SAP provides a wealth of resources to ensure a smooth transition:

  • SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Upgrade, a methodology for implementing SAP solutions, guides your team through the upgrade by prompting deliverables and tasks at the right time.  
  • SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades helps assess your upgrade efforts with analysis of your custom code, compatibility scope usage, and add-on usage.  
  • SAP guidance and resources for technical upgrade execution are included with software subscription., including tasks like operating system upgrade, database upgrade, and the technical installation of SAP S/4HANA upgrades. Please remember that as an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customer, you're responsible for planning upgrades, requesting services, and handling post-upgrade tasks.
  • The Enterprise Support Academy and dedicated SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Value Maps* help upskill your team.  
  • SAP Services and Support including SAP Enterprise Support available with all cloud offerings, and several premium plans, can bring you a dedicated expert assistant for the short or long term.
  • SAP Partner Finder can help you identify a partner from our vast ecosystem for implementation services.  

 

In conclusion: By embracing regular upgrades and a clean core approach, you unlock a multitude of benefits, including accelerated innovation adoption and increased security and compliance. Building your upgrade routine and leveraging valuable tools, services and support from SAP and its ecosystem will result in a smoother upgrade journey.

So don’t let your system reach a point where a costly and time-consuming overhaul becomes essential to avoid slowdown. Maximize the value of your SAP investment - embrace agility and upgrade to stay ahead of the competition.

Read the whitepaper, "Embrace agility and upgrade for success."

Join our Upgrade dedicated SAP Community

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team

BW (SAP Business Warehouse)

entry.title: Re: How do we use Oauth to call SOLR externally with SSO...
entry.content:
One can't use Oauth to connect to solr, as basic authentication to solr isn't supported. One cannot enable nor customize it in the security.json file. If one does, one will face deployment errors.

This is explained in following documentation:

"3320534 - How to connect Solr through cURL/Postman in SAP Commerce" https://me.sap.com/notes/3320534/E
Solr Security https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/b2f400d4c0414461a4bb7e115dccd779/f7251d5a1d6848489b1ce7ba46300fe6.html#solr-security

Similar question was asked here:
https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-q-a/how-do-we-call-solr-externally-with-sso-enabled/qaq-p/14027509#M453809

entry.title: How Data driven Change Management with Deep Transformation Insights ensures successful SAP Business
entry.content:
This blog highlights the critical role of Organizational Change Management (OCM) in the successful implementation of SAP S/4HANA. It delves into how SAP's Deep Transformation Insights (DTI) service leverages data-driven insights to help organizations manage change effectively, ensuring employee acceptance and engagement throughout the transformation process.

The Challenge of SAP S/4HANA Implementations: A Change Management Perspective

Implementing SAP S/4HANA is a transformative process that goes beyond a mere technological upgrade. It reshapes business operations, IT systems, and employee behavior while embracing a Fit-to-Standard mindset This increased focus on standards helps to reduce the time and costs associated with implementation and support, and also drives business efficiencies as well as scalability. It is SAP’s recommended approach to enable faster adoption of SAP S/4HANA.

Despite the recognized importance of Organizational Change Management (OCM) in ensuring successful business transformation, many organizations struggle to integrate it effectively into their strategy. We recently did a Change Assessment with more than 800 respondents on the perception of different important OCM related dimensions. See here some highlights:

Key Challenges in OCM Implementations

Strategic Alignment and Ownership

  • Confusion about who should take ownership of OCM within the organization
  • Lack of clarity in defining desired business outcomes
  • Misconceptions about OCM's scope and responsibilities

Leadership Involvement

  • 64% of companies acknowledge senior leaders' crucial role in driving change
  • Only 56% report active engagement of leaders with their teams
  • Lack of visible support undermines morale and alignment

Communication and Change Story

  • Only 48% of organizations actively communicate SAP program updates
  • 54% have a clearly defined Change Story

Resistance to change evolves when discussions focus around Fit-Gap keeping the current business processes in the future system and NOT focusing on fit-to-standard.

Past Experiences and Skepticism

  • Poor outcomes in previous OCM efforts lead to skepticism
  • Some organizations exclude OCM from new projects due to past experiences

Resource Constraints and ROI Measurement

  • Lack of internal structures and expertise for effective OCM implementation
  • 71% believe their Change Management Strategy explains the SAP program's importance
  • Only 47% feel end users are equipped to apply SAP solutions in daily work
  • OCM is often viewed as an optional expense rather than a critical success factor
  • Difficulty in justifying additional spending on OCM
  • Only 44% of organizations actively assess the effectiveness of their change initiatives
  • Lack of clear metrics to demonstrate ROI

Overcoming these challenges requires a holistic approach to OCM, emphasizing clear ownership, leadership involvement, effective communication, and measurable outcomes. By addressing these key areas, organizations can maximize the benefits of their SAP S/4HANA implementation and ensure a smoother transition for all stakeholders.

To address such challenges the Deep Transformation Insights (DTI) service from SAP comes into play. With this data driven approach, organizations can safeguard the success of their SAP S/4HANA implementations. It's not just about deploying new technology; it's about preparing the people who will use it, ensuring sustainable adoption, and unlocking the full potential of the transformation.

DTI is built around five key dimensions or sentiments—Awareness, Acceptance, Enablement, Empowerment, and Commitment—that provide a structured path for guiding individuals through change, fostering both readiness and long-term engagement. Let’s look at these briefly.

Research-based Change Sentiments to Accelerate Change and Adoption in SAP Implementations

Based on research conducted by organizational scientists like A. Armenakis, this five important factors have been identified as they determine the degree of employee buy-in by organizational change recipients.

Awareness

  • Foundation of successful transformation
  • Ensures recognition of the need for change
  • Focuses on communicating the "why" behind the change
  • Aligns change initiative with broader business goals

Acceptance

  • Builds on awareness
  • Addresses emotional and intellectual readiness
  • Focuses on trust-building and reducing resistance
  • Creates psychological safety for employees

Enablement

  • Bridge between readiness and action
  • Equips individuals with necessary tools, training, and resources
  • Creates a supportive environment for adapting to new roles
  • Minimizes disruption and maximizes productivity during transformation

Empowerment

  • Fosters ownership and confidence among employees
  • Grants authority for decision-making within respective roles
  • Cultivates a culture of distributed decision-making
  • Inspires innovation and accountability at all levels

Adoption and Commitment

  • Emphasizes alignment between personal and organizational goals
  • Prioritizes intrinsic and extrinsic motivation
  • Focuses on recognition and reinforcement
  • Ensures continued engagement, real productive usage of the new processes or tools

 

From an SAP perspective, we assembled these factors and change sentiments into a framework aligned with the SAP Activate methodology and focused on SAP systems implementations.

We call this framework Deep Transformation Insights (DTI), utilizing change sentiment questionnaires throughout the implementation cycle for customers. DTI contains assessment tools that can be administered at any stage of the implementation process.

The insights obtained can serve as:

(a) An indicator of the degree of adoption/acceptance among users

(b) An assessment of issues in project enablement that can adversely impact the success of the implementation

(c) A basis for planning and executing learning to enhance user adoption

(d) A regular data driven pulse-check to monitor success of actions to improve the five dimensions.

Customer Case highlighting Activities from Data-Driven Insights to Accelerate User Adoption in SAP S/4HANA Projects

So how could this look like in SAP projects? To make it tangible we would like to share a real world customer case.   

The customer prepared for a planned implementation of SAP S/4HANA supporting an enterprise-wide digital transformation strategy to enhance scalability and efficiency. Further business alignment goals included greater simplicity, efficiency, insight, collaboration, transparency, and compliance, among others.

The company achieved success by prioritizing employee buy-in and minimizing disruption. Using SAP's Deep Transformation Insights service, they enhanced their change management with digital tools and data-driven insights. This people-centric approach effectively managed the transformation's complexity, leading to a successful implementation After implementing the DTI service, the company established and orchestrated an employee engagement feedback architecture using a structured change-sentiment framework across project and organizational units.
Additionally, they gained better insights into stakeholder perceptions and experiences throughout the project lifecycle. They now use these insights as the foundation to accelerate and support solution adoption.

"Utilizing DTI was seamless, and the insights we gained from the team were very thorough, thoughtful, and actionable. We were able to easily see that the work we were doing was having the intended effect because of the results from DTI"
Director Organizational Change Management, Kyndryl

Summary

Organizational Change Management (OCM) is crucial for ensuring successful adoption and realizing the full potential of any digital transformation. However, many organizations face challenges in integrating OCM into their implementation strategy.

Deep Transformation Insights (DTI) offers a comprehensive solution to these challenges, providing a data-driven approach to change management that aligns with the SAP Activate methodology. By focusing on five proven key sentiments—Awareness, Acceptance, Enablement, Empowerment, and Commitment—DTI helps organizations guide their employees through the change process, fostering engagement and long-term success. Find out more about SAP Organizational Change Management on sap.com, or contact us per e-mail.

We look forward to discuss your questions in the comments.

entry.title: Re: Issue saving approved document drafts as document via...
entry.content:
We observed, that OWDD entries are deleted after calling "SaveDraftToDocument" ONLY WHEN the "Authorizer User" (from Approval Stages > Authoriser) is also assigned as "Originator/Requestor User" (in Approval Templates > Originator > User). Reproducible with SBO FP2405 / FP2408, both DI-Api and ServiceLayer.
It took us weeks to find the reason for deleted OWDD entries.

I hope it helps.

entry.title: SF_TIM_Employee_Timesheet_v2.0 report story
entry.content:
Hello, 

The SF_TIM_Employee_Timesheet_v2.0 report story documentation details that fields "Startdate" and "Enddate" are selected from the employee time sheet table. However, in all instances I implemented this template, the start date filter retrieves error and we cannot filter on it. 

In the screenshots we see clearly that the filter is functioning and that the fields are added to the timesheet detail tables, whereas when I import the story in the instance, the fields are not included in the tables. 

When previewing the query, these 2 fields do not appear in the columns view. 

When starting a new story, those 2 fields do not exist in the table. 

Investigating, I found this KBA : 3287193 - Start Time, End Time, and Start Date of a time recording in Time Sheet are returned empty in reports

But if I select the date from the employee time table it is either blank or an incorrect date, which do not represent the employee time sheet start date.

I have tried to change the format of the start date using this KBA: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/mimes/call.htm?number=2986093 

but then it is a text field and I cannot use it to filter.

How can I add the start and end date of the employee time sheet table? How can we work with this story without errors in the dates? It is crucial for customers to be able to filter on dates as there are so many records in Time management. 

Any help is greatly appreciated

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Does a SAC workspace functions like private files?
entry.content:
It's very confusing indeed.
But if you navigate from the 'System' folder (need manage files permission) towards the workspaces file area, you can create a new folder outside of the users folders & share it with workspace members/teams. Then it behaves like a public folder.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2502
entry.content:
Welcome to the release update blog of SAP Asset Performance Management for 2502. We're excited to share various enhancements in Asset Risk Management, Asset Reliability Engineering, Asset Data and Information Management, and Asset Health Monitoring that we believe will greatly improve your experience. Here's a list of the most important innovations of what's new:

  • Assign failure mode to Risk & Criticality Assessments
  • Displaying additional details such as 'Maintenance Plant', 'Planning Plant' on technical objects
    in Risk & Criticality Assessments 
  • Updated ‘Publish’ role in Risk & Criticality Assessments 
  • Create Recommendations without assessments with failure data
  • Enhancements to Reliability Assessments
  • Update on authorization with respect to RCM and FMEA Assessments 
  • Enable attribute-based authorization on Strategy Assessment for Classes
  • Efficiently manage your industrial system structure with new ‘Export and Import' options
  • Industrial system navigation with ‘Expand All’ and ‘Collapse All’ features
  • Download a pre-formatted Microsoft excel template to build a system hierarchy
  • Configure initial alert status and alert creation timestamps from rules
  • Enhancing rules with flexible user notifications
  • Multi-language support for rules attributes
  • Adding 4 new languages in SAP Asset Performance Management

Asset Risk Assessment

Assign failure mode to Risk & Criticality Assessments

Risk & Criticality Assessments are extended so that you can assign one or multiple  failure modes to each impact of a technical object to convey risk and potential failures. This enables you to run a failure mode centric paradigm in end-to-end asset management.

Key features

  • Display linked catalog profile in the Assignments tab.
  • Assign one or multiple failure modes against each impact assessed: Provides an F4 help selection field in the Assessments tab to select one or more failure modes per impact.
  • Apply To option: If all technical objects have the same catalog profile, the failure mode is assigned or copied only once when assessing multiple objects or when using the 'Apply To' option.

For more information, see Assessing Asset Criticality Based on the Risk Score.

Picture: When performing a Risk & Criticality Assessment, you can assign Failure Mode on each Impact by using F4 help.


Displaying additional details such as Maintenance Plant, Planning Plant on technical objects in Risk & Criticality Assessments 

Risk & Criticality Assessments now display additional details pertaining to the technical objects in the ‘Assignment’ tab. The additional details shown are

  • Technical Object Status
  • Equipment/Functional Location Category
  • Object Type
  • Structure in which the Technical Object is part of or installed
  • Maintenance Plant
  • Planning Plant

These additional details are also supported with ‘Export/Import’ of Risk and Criticality Assessments.

Updated ‘Publish’ role in Risk & Criticality Assessments 

We’re enhancing the authorization for Risk & Criticality Assessments. It is designed to differentiate the authorization on user roles so that it clear task separation is possible. Users with the RiskCriticalityAssessment_Publish  role can:

  • Release assessments.
  • Mark assessments as Obsolete.
  • Revert Obsolete status to previous state.


Asset Reliability Engineering

Assign failure data to Recommendations without assessments

We’re extending ‘Manage Recommendations’ application by enabling creation of Recommendations without assessments with failure data such as failure modes, failure effects and causes. This enhancement is part of our commitment to enable failure mode centric paradigm in end-to-end Asset Management.

Key features

  • Enable failure data assignment for recommendations created without assessments
  • Assign one or multiple failure modes, failure effects, and causes
  • Search, filter and list the recommendations based on failure modes

Enhancements on Reliability Assessments

We’re providing continuous enhancements on Reliability Assessments to improve usability. In release 2502, you can experience the followings. 

Key features

  • Support filtering of Strategy Assessment for Classes based on operating context and condition
  • UI improvement to Strategy Assessment for Classes (Similar to RCM/FMEA Assessments)
  • Provide free texts/notes while building failure data hierarchy in reliability assessments

Picture: You can filter Strategy Assessments for Classes per Operating Context and Condition.

Update on authorization for RCM and FMEA Assessments 

After the 2505 release, the permissions for releasing assessments will change. Users with the FMEAAssessments_Create and FMEAAssessments_Update roles will no longer have access. Instead, users with the FMEAAssessments_Publish role will be granted the ability to release assessments. For more information, see 3567655 

The same applies to RCM Assessments. Users with the RCMAssessments_Create and RCMAssessments_Update roles will no longer have access. Instead, users with the RCMAssessments_Publish role will be granted the ability to release assessments. For more information, see 3567615. Information published on SAP site.

  • FMEAAssessments_Publish role: See 3567655 
  • RCMAssessments_Publish role: See 3567615 

Enable attribute-based authorization on Strategy Assessment for Classes

‘Manage Strategy Assessment for Classes’ introduces attribute-based authorization based on ‘Planning Plant’ and ‘Maintenance Plant’ fields. This attribute-based authorization checks to ensure that information is only accessible to authorized users.

Key features

  • Update: Only users authorized for the specific Planning Plant and Maintenance Plant will be able to update these fields.
  • View: Users without authorization for the specific Planning Plant and Maintenance Plant combination will be able to view the assessment and recommendations. This allows you to review the information before deciding whether to proceed with copying.

This provides the necessary authorization and effective controls to support central and regional Strategy Assessment for Classes as Templates and Assessments.

Picture: Without having the relevant planning plant and maintenance plant assigned, it will not be possible to update the strategy assessment for classes. 

Asset Data and Information Management

Efficiently manage your industrial system structure with new ‘Export and Import’ options

Managing complex industrial system hierarchies is now easier than ever with our new Export and Import options available in the System Structure dropdown under the Structure tab. This functionality is designed to save time and enhance efficiency by streamlining the process of building and integrating system hierarchies.

Key Features

  • Export system structure: Build and export the hierarchy of your industrial systems. This feature allows for a structured and detailed view, facilitating the sharing and documentation of system configurations.
  • Import system structure: Seamlessly import existing industrial system hierarchies using Excel files. This capability ensures quick integration of system data, maintaining consistency and reducing manual entry errors.:

Previously, building an industrial system required manually creating each system and its elements one by one. Now, with the "Import System Structure" feature, the process is streamlined

  1. Initiating import
    Navigate to the "Import System Excel" option, click "Browse" to select your Excel file containing the system hierarchy details, and start the import process.
  2. Asynchronous processing
    The Job Manager handles the import asynchronously. You can track the status as "In Process," "Completed," or identify any issues if marked as "Failed." This transparency ensures you are informed of the process at each stage.
  3. Automatic system creation
    Upon completion, the system automatically creates the entire hierarchy using the imported Excel data. This includes all general information, language settings, plant details, and system elements. The tree structure with top nodes, functional hierarchies, products, and locations is compiled, offering a comprehensive view of the entire system.
  4. Verification and expansion
    Users can expand the hierarchy to view detailed node information and verify the accuracy of the imported data. This feature enables you to confirm that the system reflects the intended structure and details.

These export and import capabilities streamline system management, reduce errors, and save time, making it easier to maintain accurate industrial system hierarchies. For more details, see the SAP Help Portal Export or Import a System Structure.

Enhance industrial system navigation with ‘Expand All’ and ‘Collapse All’ features

Navigating through complex industrial system hierarchies has become simpler with our new Expand All and Collapse All buttons. These tools allow users to quickly view or minimize their entire system structure, enhancing efficiency and ease of navigation.

Key Features

  • Expand all and collapse all buttons
    These buttons, located in the structure tab, provide a quick and comprehensive view of your entire system hierarchy or simplify your workspace by collapsing all nodes. This is perfect for quickly locating specific system elements, especially after page reloads or re-navigation.
  • Seamless functionality with large data sets
    The system is designed to manage both small and extensive data hierarchies effectively, ensuring that users can expand or collapse structures effortlessly, even when handling a significant amount of data.

Picture: Please see above screen shot.

Download a pre-formatted Microsoft excel template to build a system hierarchy

Our latest feature simplifies the process of organizing and managing industrial system hierarchies through a downloadable, pre-formatted Microsoft excel template. Accessible via the "Download Template" button on the list page, this tool is designed to enhance efficiency and organization.

Key Features

  • Structured design: The template offers a structured approach to building a hierarchy of your industrial systems, ensuring consistency and accuracy across your data.
  • Comprehensive sheets: The Excel file includes multiple sheets to cover all aspects of your system's hierarchy:
    • System Header Details: Capture essential details of your system.
    • Top Node: Define the principal node of your hierarchy.
    • System Element Hierarchy (or Aspect Hierarchy): Organize the elements or aspects within your system.
    • Relationships (or Product Individual): Map out relationships or individual product details.
    • Product Individual: Detail each unique product within your hierarchy.

For more detailed guidance on managing industrial systems using the template, see the SAP Help Portal Manage Industrial Systems.

Asset Health Monitoring

Configure alert status and alert creation date from rules

‘Manage Rules’ in release 2502 introduces new enhancements designed to give you greater control over your alert workflows. With the latest updates, you now have the ability to configure the initial alert status and alert creation timestamp directly from your monitoring rules, making your processes more efficient and tailored to your needs. (* APM based on embedded IoT only)

Key features

  1. Alert status customization
    With the new 'Alert Status' field, you can set whether alerts generated by a rule should begin in the 'New' status or 'In Process' status. This flexibility allows you to align alerts with your team’s workflow, ensuring that alerts are handled appropriately from the moment they're created. Based on this it is for example possible to create alerts in status 'In Process' right from the beginning in case a parallel notification has been created at the same time.

  2. Control alert creation date
    The 'Alert Creation Date' feature gives you the power to specify the timestamp for alerts generated by rules. This control for example ensures that alert creation timestamps accurately reflect the timing of the conditions that triggered their creation, enhancing your ability to track and respond to events.

Picture: Use control + Space to get suggestions in the fields for Alert Status and Alert Creation Date. 

For detailed instructions on how to implement these features, see the SAP Help Portal Creating a Monitoring Rule

Enhancing rule management with flexible user notifications

We’re providing users with greater flexibility in receiving and managing notifications. The introduction of the "User Notification" feature is designed to streamline communication and improve workflow efficiency by providing customizable notification options for your rules.
(*APM based on embedded IoT only)

Key Features

  • Customizable notification body: Note in email notification’s body is fully customizable including dynamic values and hyperlinks. This flexibility ensures that emails convey the necessary context and details for recipients to take appropriate action.
  • Default email body include clickable hyperlinks to created alert and affected technical object.
  • Recipient selection: Users must select notification recipients from those maintained within the Identity provider associated with APM. This ensures alerts are directed to the correct individuals and maintains compliance with user management policies.
  • Flexible delivery options: Notifications can be delivered as FLP notifications or as emails, depending on user preferences. This flexibility allows users to choose the most convenient mode of alert delivery, enhancing usability and reducing notification fatigue.
  • User-controlled subscription: Users can manage their notification preferences, deciding whether to receive alerts as FLP notifications, emails, or a combination of both. This control over alert delivery ensures that users receive notices in a way that best fits their workflow and priorities.

Multi-language support for rules attributes

Our latest update provides a practical solution for organizations operating in multilingual environments by allowing users to translate all attributes used in rule conditions. This new feature supports several additional languages, including Italian, Indonesian, and Norwegian, among others.
(*APM based on embedded IoT only)

  • Improved accessibility: Translating data set attributes ensures that users can interact with rule conditions in their native languages, reducing misunderstandings and improving data management accuracy.
  • Enhanced collaboration: This update supports better collaboration among international teams by enabling everyone to work in their preferred language, fostering clearer communication and efficient workflows.

By clicking the translation button within the data set interface, users can easily add translations for rule condition attributes. This simple process helps tailor the system to meet diverse language needs, ensuring clarity for all team members.

Supporting 4 new languages across SAP Asset Performance Management

As mentioned above in Rules, more languages are added in SAP Asset Performance Management in the release 2502. This applies to all other apps across Asset Risk Management, Reliability Engineering, and Asset Data and Information Management. These languages will be supported in both short and long texts (UI & APIs), including Export / Import excel.

  • Norwegian
  • Dutch
  • Italian
  • Indonesian

This is the release highlights I prepared for the release 2502. Hope this provides you good overview. You can also refer to live webcast or recordings to get the details.

You can subscribe/register the webcast sessions as below.
SAP Digital Supply Chain Engagement Hub - Intelligent Asset Management & Field Service Management

Keep In Touch

Here's a quick reminder how to keep in touch with all things APM:

  • Check out all past and present Intelligent Asset Management webcasts here. If you register for an IAM webcast, you will be automatically subscribed to our IAM Community Engagement Newsletter. 
  • Check some of our recent blogs:
    • What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2501 - SAP Community
    • Why Failure-Mode Focus Matters in Intelligent Asset Management 
    • SAP Asset Performance Management Embedding Cumulocity IoT to Drive Innovations in IoT and AI
    • How AI-based Anomaly Detection empowers reliability engineers in SAP Asset Performance Management
  • Subscribe to What's New on the SAP Help Portal
  • Submit or vote for the improvement you would like to see in the SAP Continuous Influence session

entry.title: Critical Success Factors for a Seamless SAP S/4HANA Transition
entry.content:
For many companies, the move to SAP S/4HANA is a strategic step toward digital transformation. The benefits are clear—real-time analytics, streamlined processes, and a modern user interface. However, the transition often turns out to be more complex than initially expected.

From my experience with SAP S/4HANA projects, these are some of the most critical factors for success:

  1. Choosing the Right Migration Approach

The first major decision is the migration strategy. There is no one-size-fits-all approach, but selecting the right path early on is crucial:

  • Greenfield: A complete system redesign, ideal for companies looking to optimize processes but requiring significant change management.
  • Brownfield: A system conversion that retains existing structures, making the transition smoother but limiting flexibility.
  • Selective Data Transition: A hybrid approach that allows companies to retain critical historical data while restructuring processes where needed.

Many companies struggle with this choice. What has worked best in your experience?

  1. Process Harmonization and the Role of SAP Standard

An SAP S/4HANA project is more than just a technical migration—it is an opportunity to rethink processes. A key question in every project is:

  • How much standardization is feasible?
  • Which custom developments are truly necessary?

The closer companies stay to the SAP standard, the easier future upgrades become, and the lower the long-term maintenance effort. However, business-specific requirements must also be carefully considered.

  1. Data Migration – A Critical Success Factor

Data quality often becomes a major roadblock in SAP S/4HANA projects. Missing, duplicate, or inconsistent data can lead to significant delays and operational disruptions. A well-defined data cleansing strategy and thorough test migrations are essential to ensure a smooth go-live.

  1. Change Management and Training

A successful transition to SAP S/4HANA is not just about technology—it’s also about people. Many companies underestimate the impact of change on end users. The most successful projects focus on:

  • Early communication about what is changing and why
  • Comprehensive training programs for key users and end users
  • Post-go-live support to address issues quickly and ensure adoption

Without a structured change management approach, even the most technically sound implementation can face resistance from users.

  1. Leveraging SAP BTP and Automation

SAP S/4HANA provides a solid foundation, but many companies look beyond the core system to drive additional value. The SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) enables:

  • Fiori applications for an improved user experience
  • AI-driven automation to reduce manual effort
  • Cloud and hybrid integration through the SAP Integration Suite

The challenge is to determine which innovations truly add value without adding unnecessary complexity.

Final Thoughts

Moving to SAP S/4HANA is not just an IT project—it is a business transformation. Companies that carefully evaluate their migration strategy, focus on data quality, embrace standardization where possible, and invest in change management are the ones that achieve long-term success.

What have been your biggest challenges in SAP S/4HANA projects? How did you approach them? I’d love to hear your insights.

entry.title: Offer Letter Conditional Tokens
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

We are implementing a conditional token to our offer letters.

We have two languages in the system (en_US and de_DE) and the same conditional token should resolve to different parts of text based on the language of the offer letter template and the value of a picklist field from offer approval.

However, when we trigger the offer letter the token does not resolve. We have created a rule trace and seems that the issue is here: 'Compare 'null' with 'en_US' by operator 'EQUAL'. Result: 'false'.' The language of offer letter is considered 'null' and, as a result, the condition is false.  Here is a screenshot from system when the user selects the offer letter:

If we delete the condition for the language and just keep the picklist condition, then the token resolves but we will have the same text for en_US and de_DE.

Have you ever implemented conditional tokens that should resolve to different parts of text based on offer letter language and can share a solution?

Any suggestions are highly appreciated!

Many thanks in advance,

Leandra

entry.title: Re: Installation of equipment to functional location via...
entry.content:
Hi adrakev,

The simple answer is no - it is not possible.

The way of doing it is as follows:
1. You create a Corrective Maintenance order, release it.
2. Technician starts the physical work, de-installs/installs new equipment etc.
3. Technician performs time confirmations and spare parts GI (if relevant) against this maint. order in SAP.
4. Technician/Maintenance Supervisor de-installs/installs new equipment in SAP via IE4N. As a reference, the corrective maintenance order from point 1. is used. 

Alternatively - if your case does not require inventory movements, equipment can be 'deinstalled/installed' in master data. You go to the equipment, structure tab and install it there under specific FLOC or superior equipment. However, if your assets are also stock managed, then IE4N is the only way of doing this.

Best,
Dan

entry.title: Experience SAP Solutions in Action - Navigating the Energy Transition at EUC 2025
entry.content:
Dear Energy and Utilities Community,

The time has come once again! This year, we are delighted to invite you to the SAP Energy and Utilities Conference (EUC) in the dynamic city of Rotterdam, from April 8th to 10th.

The conference will feature a lineup of talks from esteemed customers, valued partners, and inspiring thought leaders. At our SAP booth in the exhibition, attendees will have the opportunity to gain valuable insights into the topics shaping our industries today.

Building on the increased interest we've seen in our demo booth over the past three years, we're delighted to offer a wide array of solution demos once again at the SAP booth.

Our SAP industry experts will be on hand to showcase these solutions, answer your questions, provide personalized advice, and offer deep insights into the innovative solutions SAP brings to the table.

Curious about what awaits you at our booth? You can book the relevant demos in the Event App which will be launched around mid-March.

 

 

SAP Booth @ EUC 2024 – Seville, Spain.

Utilities Demos:

Asset Management & Supply Chain

  • RISE with SAP for Utilities – Enterprise Asset Management: The demo focuses on the operation and maintenance phase of the asset life cycle. Utilities have thousands of assets in the field and in the plants, and the performance of these assets is a critical part of their business. Technology can be used to monitor these assets and predict problems before they occur or before they become critical. The demo shows how an error in a transformer is detected early enough based on sensor data and then repaired by the field crew before an outage occurs. This includes the analysis of the sensor data, the triggering and planning of a work order, the purchase of required spare parts, scheduling of work, the actual work on the field, and analyzing the efforts. 

 

  • Smart Power Plant Demo: Experience live the insights of the SAP Smart Power Plant from the SAP Walldorf campus.

 

  • Geo Framework with SAP Enterprise Product Development Visualization of a Transformer Station: Users can leverage map-driven technology to streamline decision-making by seamlessly integrating business assets, such as equipment and maintenance orders, with geographic information systems (GIS). Through the Geographical Enablement Framework, companies can enhance efficiency and connectivity by enriching SAP Business Suite and SAP S/4HANA solutions with spatial awareness features, bridging the gap between GIS and business applications. This integration facilitates smoother workflows, empowering users to drive business processes directly from the map interface, thus achieving true data federation and optimizing decision-making capabilities.

 

  • Intelligent Asset Management e2e demo: a demo that walks through end-to-end process for Intelligent Asset Management solutions (SAP Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management, SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration, SAP Asset Performance Management, SAP S/4HANA Asset Management for Resource Scheduling, SAP Field Service Management, SAP Service and Asset Manager).

 

  • Scheduling / Mobile Service and Asset Management: SAP Service and Asset Manager enables maintenance technicians to view their assigned work orders and perform work order confirmations on a mobile device (iPad). Maintenance technicians can also create new work orders and/or notifications on their own to report additional findings and trigger follow-up processes. In this case all relevant steps will be performed on the mobile device.

 

  • From Request to Resolution: Faster Meter Swaps with SAP Field Service Management: Efficient meter installations and exchanges require seamless coordination between customers, technicians, and back-office teams. SAP Field Service Management optimizes the process with AI-driven scheduling, mobile execution, and automated compliance documentation—enhancing efficiency, reducing delays, and improving customer satisfaction.

 

Customer Experience

  • SAP Service Cloud Version 2: Numerous trends are pushing utilities companies to adopt more customer-centric approaches and provide seamless and personalized service. Experience the SAP Service Cloud Utilities add-on to learn how you can orchestrate utilities processes directly from SAP Service Cloud​. Watch the video.

 

  • Customer Service processes in the Customer Engagement: The interaction center agents can use the interaction center as a central point of entry to search and manage the Utilities master data as well as to carry out the traditional Utilities industry processes such as meter reading, bill correction, move-in/out and more. This demo is a walkthrough of features and functions available with this solution to help Utilities CSR to perform his / her job effectively.

 

  • SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement – Marketing Automation for Utilities: Accelerate time to value, meet customers where they are with what they want, and produce measurable results with real-time, scalable personalization.

 

Energy & Markets

  • SAP Cloud for Energy: Get to know SAP Cloud for Energy, the platform for the complete metering data lifecycle management. Check out these videos Operational Monitoring, Solve Issues, Device Master Data, Configuration.

 

  • Managing the increasing complexity of metered installations in a world of Distributed Energy Resources with the Measurement Concept Management: SAP Cloud for Utilities foundation, also known as Measurement Concept Management, or MCM for short, is a new SAP solution aimed mainly at the distributed generation market. The solution serves to enable customers to manage, streamline and unify processes around installations including decentralized energy resources throughout their whole lifecycle, from their creation to the final shutdown. Read more about SAP Cloud for Utilities foundation here:

 

  • SAP Walldorf Energy Park: SAP executing on the Energy Transition: The SAP Energy Park initiative aims to not only transform or own operations but also empower other organizations to embrace the energy transition through a combination of cutting-edge technology, innovative real-life scenarios, and strategic partnerships.

 

  • BeFlexible: European Flexibility Management Platform: BeFlexible is the innovation project for the Digital Network and Platform for power grids within the European CEO Alliance. It aims at increasing the flexibility of the energy system, improving the cooperation between DSOs TSOs, facilitating the participation of all energy-related stakeholders, enabling to build non-energy services.

 

  • From Quote to Install – Streamlining Solar PV Projects with SAP Field Service Management: Utilities are expanding into renewable energy services, including photovoltaic (PV) system installations. SAP Field Service Management simplifies the end-to-end process—from scheduling site assessments for quote generation to coordinating multi-stage installations and managing external contractors. With AI-driven planning, mobile tools for technicians, and seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA, utilities can efficiently deliver solar projects while ensuring a smooth customer experience.

 

  • E-Mobility - Billing & operations of EV charging services: 
    • EV charging point management with SAP E-Mobility.
    • EV fleet charging management and EV charging solutions for condominiums
    • Efficient and integrated billing of EV charging cards and charging solution offerings with SAP Subscription Billing and S/4HANA.
    • Billing & operations of EV charging services with SAP: Build, run, and manage electric vehicle charging networks with integration into end-to-end billing processes on one platform. Get more details.

 

SAP S/4HANA Utilities / SAP Utilities Core

  • SAP Utilities Core: Meter to Cash and New Connection Process: SAP Utilities Core is our solution offering addressing the core Utilities industry meter-to-cash process including customer service and market process management. Read more here 

 

  • Machine Learning for implausible meter readings and outsorted billing documents: The demo showcases the specific capabilities of machine learning-based processing for implausible meter readings. It is seamlessly integrated in meter reading processing in IS-U, learns from the meter reading and consumption history, and provides the capability to determine a machine learning release confidence used to automate the release of implausible meter readings.

 

  • Automatic Implausible MR Correction using Business Rules (BRFplus): One of the key processes for a Utility is the meter to cash process, which starts from the reading of the meter, billing and invoicing, and collecting the payment. At each step of the process, there are validation rules put in place to ensure the correct data is captured, billed and collected from the customer. The Demo focuses on the first step of the process capturing discrete meter reads from external systems and performing validations. This function is part of the SAP S/4HANA Utilities solution and is executed by Device Management.

 

  • SAP FIORI Apps Billing and Meter Reading: See the new user experience for dedicated metering and billing roles and the possibilities to branch from KPI-bar charts through list views into a detailed single customer case.

 

  • Business Process Exception Management - KPI Designer: The focus of this Demo will be utilizing the Business Process Exception Management (BPEM) data to illustrate how the application can improve the management of exceptions. Charts and tables will be created to view data. Semantic objects will be defined to provide the ability to execute business processes and/or transactions against the BPEM data without logging into another application.

 

  • How to boost your extra commodity business with an end-2-end order to cash: In the digital economy, the order-to-cash process is a critical driver of business success, especially for companies offering subscription-based products. Addressing the complexities of this process requires a sophisticated approach to manage product lifecycle, billing, and financial operations efficiently. Our Order-to-Cash Solution, l is crafted to optimize the entire lifecycle of subscription products. This transformative solution encompasses comprehensive billing, invoice consolidation, and financial management, underpinned by advanced features such as complex calculation models and wallet concepts.

 

  • Empower and optimizing your bundling and consolidation for your extra commodity business: In response to the growing demand for sustainable energy solutions and the seamless integration of renewable technology into consumers' lifestyles, our company is excited to present a groundbreaking business initiative focused on extra commodities such as Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging and/or Solar Panels scenarios through a subscription billing model. This innovative approach aims to eliminate the financial and logistical barriers traditionally associated with acquiring and maintaining such technologies, while promoting eco-friendly, energy-efficient living.

 

Water

  • Smart Water Platform for Remote Water Quality Monitoring and Leakage Detection: Get insights on this platform watching this video

 

  • SAP Utilities Core for Water: Meter to cash for Water platform in SAP S/4HANA: Reduce architecture complexity and gain operational efficiency moving into a single. Get some insights watching this video

 

Utilities Analytics

  • S.MART Energy Shop: Journey from Energy Consumer to Prosumer: SAP and partners solutions provide the right capabilities for Utilities to guide their customers to become and active member of the energy transition, leading to a more decentralized, digitalized and sustainable energy system.

 

  • Analytics, Billing and Meter Reading: Learn how to easily configure your own individualized report- based standard analytical scenarios in SAP S/4HANA Utilities.

 

  • SAP S/4HANA Utilities Content for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere

 

Oil, Gas & Energy Demos:

  • Shutdown Turnaround & Outage Solution for Refineries by Wipro: This solution jointly developed with WIPRO as our partner where you will see comprehensive and integrated STO management solution. Which addresses customer challenges as diverse as end-to-end STO processes, scope management, non-integrated planning, long lead items tracking, safety, scheduling, real-time effective reporting, control of costs, risks, procurement, and user-friendliness.

 

  • SAP Field Logistics Management - Efficiently manage Upstream remote logistics: In this demo, customers can see end to end process of how the SAP Field Logistics solution helps to manage the movement of products required for maintenance operations between a supplying base and a remote location.

 

  • Follow the molecule throughout the commodity value chain using SAP Solution CTRM & Hydrocarbon supply chain Solution: End-to-end visibility of the commodity value chain delivering natively integrated capabilities incorporating Trading, Risk Management, Scheduling and Settlement with focus on margin and inventory precision.
  • Further demos we are going to show - stayed tuned for a detailed information:
    • Predictive Maintenance Demo
    • Digital Twins driving the future of Business
    • SAP Prepayment Solution
    • Secondary Distribution for Oil, Gas, and Energy
    • Retail Fuel Network Operations
    • Joint Venture Accounting - Joint Interest Billing
    • Primary Distribution for Oil, Gas, & Energy

Cross-Industry Demos:

  • SAP Sustainability Control Tower - Holistic Steering and Reporting: Experience a comprehensive approach to sustainability management with our SAP Sustainability Control Tower demo. This interactive showcase will illustrate how organizations can effectively steer, track, and report their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) initiatives.

 

  • SAP Green Token: Stakeholders want to know the origin and sustainability of their products. SAP Green Token supports material traceability and transparency with segregation or mass balancing, tokenization, and a blockchain-based chain of custody for trust and compliance with recognized standards.

 

  • SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, and Quote) for B2C and B2B Offers and Quotations: See how SAP CPQ handles complex configuration of products and comprehensive quotation processes.

 

Business AI for Energy and Utilities:

  • Increasing User Productivity with SAP Joule: We are excited to introduce you to Joule, your new virtual bestie, embedded in SAP Solutions. Joule makes every task more interactive, fun and - most importantly - makes SAP Solutions even easier to use. Explore now, how Joule can help to increase user productivity within SAP Solutions.

 

  • Create Job Descriptions using SAP Joule: Explore now, how Joule embedded in SAP SuccessFactors can support recruiters and hiring managers to create job description effectively, to make a hiring process more efficient and to find the right candidate in a short period of time!

 

  • Intelligent CX with SAP CX AI Toolkit - Proactive and Textual Intelligence: The CX AI Toolkit provides several users with insights and Gen-AI use cases that boost productivity across Sales, Service, and Commerce. On top of that it is highly flexible to be designed around your specific business requirements. Visit us for a demo and see the solution in action.

 

  • How to improve your customer financial management with new and improved AI capabilities: In the era of digital transformation, organizations are increasingly turning to artificial intelligence (AI) to optimize and enhance financial operations. Our latest solution focuses on revolutionizing the contract account receivable and payable processes through the integration of AI-driven features such as behavior insights, cash application, and dispute management. This demo showcases how these technologies can streamline financial workflows, improve cash flow, and enhance decision-making, ultimately contributing to better financial health and operational efficiency.

 

  • Business AI use cases and accelerators for Oil, Gas and Energy:
    • Abnormal behavior prediction in SAP Analytics
    • Zero touch for PRA
    • Digital Assistant for Ownership Portal

 

  • GenAI-based Digital Agent for Customers Self-Service for Utilities: A conversation-driven self-service agent for utility customers that offers an alternative to traditional self-service portals. It delivers a complete experience through an interactive, natural language interface with a chat-like design, enhancing user engagement and efficiency.
  • Further planned demos - stay tuned for more details:
    • Resolving implausible meter readings automatically with AI in SAP S/4HANA Utilities
    • SAP Market Communication for Utilities, AI-assisted CONTRL-message explanation
    • GenAI-enabled Commodity Deal Capture
    • Where is my stuff

 

SAP Signavio:

  • Leverage SAP's Toolchain and Utilities content to accelerate your transformation: Get a hands-on view on how SAP's powerful toolchain, combined with industry-specific content, accelerates transformation projects for utilities. We'll dive into process analysis and modeling with SAP Signavio, landscape management with LeanIX, and implementation best practices using SAP CALM.

"In case you haven't registered for our event yet, please register here.

 

 

 

We are looking forward to meeting you in Rotterdam!

KR
Andreas Winter

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS3: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry?
entry.content:
All good things come in threes – therefore please find below (continuing my series of my previous SAP Community blog posts listed at the bottom) an overview of the main innovations dedicated to the utilities industry that are new with SAP S/4HANA FPS3 2023, the third feature pack available for SAP S/4HANA release 2023 since end of February 2025:

  1. Once more the latest feature pack of SAP S/4HANA Utilities offers further modern and flexible analytical capabilities: In continuation of previously delivered roadmap items, you can now extract energy data management (EDM) master data for reporting in SAP Datasphere such as profile headers and profile assignments. For that purpose new CDS views are  available, for details see Energy data management master data for reporting in SAP Datasphere for the utilities industry.
    The second delivered road map item in the area of analytics is Native sales statistics and unbilled revenue reporting in SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud. Existing processes and capabilities, such as statistics groups and their assignments, ODP data sources, ODP delta queue, simulation period, and IS-U mass simulation index are being reused. For more technical details please read SAP note 3473213.

  2. Several enhancements are available related to the topics Sales Interface Integration and Utilities Product Integration Layer (UPIL), and mainly targeting retailers in liberalized energy markets:
    • Period control and APIs for life cycle events and contract management processes allows you to define and calculate durations and contract periods using product attributes. These appointments are shown in the utilities sales contract overview as part of S/4HANA Utilities for customer engagement

      Moreover new OData APIs are available to read utilities sales contracts and simulate pricing for utilities. For details see APIs for Utilities Products, Utilities Sales Contracts and Utilities Sales Contract Processes.

    • Streamlining contract migration and bulk data processing offers the ability to migrate relevant parts of the contract history to utilities sales contracts. This history is being shown in utilities sales contract overview, where you can also cancel selected utilities sales contract items in case this is needed after migration.
    • In the context of the Utilities Product Integration Layer (UPIL) and the Sales Integration Interface, it is now possible to use for instance a characteristic for a product-determined price amount to a sales product for a quantity-based price. You can maintain prices, amounts etc. directly on product level. For all details see Extending pricing capabilities.

  3. Once more we have also incorporated enhancements to SAP S/4HANA Utilities based on improvement requests submitted by utility companies from various countries within our SAP Continuous Influence session dedicated to SAP S/4HANA Utilities. For all details see my blog post SAP Continuous Influence Session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities: Status Update January 2025  .

  4. Following the developer extensibility element of SAP's Clean Core strategy, several additional cloud compliant business add-ins (BAdIs) are now available for the most used SAP enhancements (aka user-exits) in IS-U. Please read SAP note 3406389 for a comprehensive overview on Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Utilities, review the list of related SAP notes.
     
  5. Finally the SAP Fiori app  Business Partner Financial Overview now includes a new facet "IS-U Invoicing Documents" showing utilities-industry specific data



    For details see Display of utilities invoices and budget billing plans in a business partner financial overview .

  6. During the last months, the central SAP for Utilities landing page on SAP Help Portal has been restructured and updated in order to provide a comprehensive overview about SAP S/4HANA Utilities and other available SAP solutions dedicated to the utilities industry. Follow https://help.sap.com/utilities to explore all details.

Finally, as a reference, here ist the list of my previous blog posts related to SAP S/4HANA 2023:

SAP S/4HANA 2023: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry? 
SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry? 
SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry?

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Andreas 

First of all thanks a lot for this blog to get us all started on the AI / ML journey! I tried to follow the steps you outlined as is, and I have the following questions, hope you can enlighten.

1) When I include massive=True parameter in AdditiveModelForecast, it gives me error that says 'feature not supported: Cannot use local temporary table'  leaving it blank as in AdditiveModelForecast(), then there is no error. What is the significance of this massive=True, and is it functionally ok to execute this method without this parameter?

2) After my AdditiveModelForecast() call, I call the fit() command with group_key, as in:

amf = AdditiveModelForecast()
amf.fit(data=df_rem_agg, group_key='MATNR') 
 
But actually, my data model looks like this:
MATNR,CATEGORY,ERDAT,FKIMG_CARTON
000000000013204583,ASORT1,20241004,0.250000000000000
000000000013204538,ASORT2,20240829,0.400000000000000
000000000013204538,ASORT2,20240829,0.400000000000000
000000000013204538,ASORT2,20240829,0.400000000000000
000000000013204537,ASORT3,20240829,0.222000000000000
000000000013204537,ASORT3,20240829,0.300000000000000
 
Means I want to group by MATNR and CATEGORY for the time series model, and not just MATNR, but the group_key only accepts string, not array. I thought I can enter it as group_key=['MATNR','CATEGORY']
 
Is this a limitation, or there is another way of defining this based on two dimensions(MATNR and CATEGORY)?
 
3) There is a reference to df_rem_countries but I don't see it defined anywhere, so I'm kind of stuck.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to change target mapping parameters for a fiori app?
entry.content:
Hi,

If you need to change parameters in a SAP-standard target mapping it will break the reference to the original catalog.

My suggestion is to make your own copy of the SAP-standard target mapping to your own technical catalog.

There you can change the parameters as you see fit and then reference this target mapping from your business catalog.

Hopefully that helps.

Nils

entry.title: Seamless Integration with SAP CI: Mastering Pagination for Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter
entry.content:
Continuing my SAP CI Pagination blog series, this time with Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter!

When integrating with Microsoft Dynamics CRM, handling large datasets efficiently is key to ensuring smooth data synchronization. Unlike Salesforce, which relies on SOQL and nextRecordsUrl, Dynamics CRM follows an OData-based pagination approach using <odata.nextLink> to fetch records in manageable batches.

In this blog, I’ll cover:
1. How pagination works in the Microsoft Dynamics CRM adapter
2. Best practices to optimize API calls and avoid performance bottlenecks
3.A step-by-step approach to implementing custom pagination in SAP CI

If you missed the first part on Salesforce Adapter, check it out here Seamless Integration with SAP CI: Mastering Pagination for Salesforce Adapter 

Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter - Handling Large Data Sets with Pagination

In this use case, we focus on retrieving records from the accounts object in Microsoft Dynamics CRM, which contains 10K+ records. To ensure efficient data retrieval, we utilize Advanced OData Queries along with the pagination feature in the receiver adapter.

Fetching data page by page, processing each batch, and then moving to the next page is the best approach for handling large datasets. In this blog, I will explain the end-to-end batching process, ensuring seamless data synchronization while optimizing API performance.

Design Approach: 

The scenario involves sending accounts object data from Microsoft Dynamics CRM to a target system that has a batch limit of 500 records per request. To accommodate this constraint, we implement a pagination approach using two Microsoft Dynamics CRM receiver adapter call.

The call fetches the page of records within a looping process to retrieve the pages one after the other. Each fetched page is passed through message mapping, ensuring seamless transformation before being delivered to the target system. This cycle continues—fetching a page, processing it, and sending it to the target—until all records are transferred, ensuring an efficient full end to end Batching processing and controlled data flow from Microsoft Dynamics CRM to the target system.

 

Design components elaboration:

Timer : This is a timer based flow which be a scheduled as per the user's input

Content Modifier : "set properties" - This step is used to accept inputs such as ObjectName, fieldnames, StartDate from the user, all these fields are externalized to make it dynamic. 

Looping Process Call : "Initiate MS Dynamics Call" -  This Looping Process Call continuously runs until the <odata.nextLink> field is absent from the response payload. The condition for looping is:

${property.NextPageLink} != ''

Here’s how it works:
-- The NextPageLink property is extracted from the response payload.
-- If NextPageLink exists, it is used in the next request to fetch the next set of records.
-- The loop continues until <odata.nextLink> is no longer present, indicating the last page.

This ensures efficient batch processing, fetching all records page by page while maintaining seamless data flow.

The Microsoft Dynamics CRM receiver adapter is placed inside the Looping Process Call to fetch records page by page.

Processing Tab

-- Pagination is enabled, ensuring controlled data retrieval.
-- Page Size is set to "500", based on the specific requirement.
-- The Next Page Link option is assigned the value "${property.NextPageLink}", which dynamically comes from the response payload.

We are not digging into the connection tab in this blog, this blog focuses only on pagination concept.

Output:

Page 1 :  Response from the Microsoft Dynamics CRM system contains 500 records and "odata.nextLink" which has the URL to the next page.

Page 2: Response from the Microsoft Dynamics CRM system contains 500 records and "odata.nextLink" which has the URL to the next page.

Content Modifier : "Read nextURL" - This is a crucial step where are we reading key field —<odata.nextLink> using xpath.

Process Call : "Message Mapping" - 

After fetching each page, the data is passed to Message Mapping, where it is processed before being sent to the target system. This step is target-specific and is not covered in this blog.

For demonstration purposes, I have included this step to mimic the "send to target" operation after message mapping. The key focus of this blog remains on pagination handling in the Microsoft Dynamics CRM adapter and ensuring seamless data retrieval.

 

Final Page Handling: Completing the Pagination Process

For a total of 10573 records, the pagination process spans 22 pages:

  • First 21 pages contain 500 records each.
  • Page 22 contains the remaining 73 records.

When the 22th page is fetched, the response contains:

  • No <odata.nextLink> , confirming that no more pages are available.

At this point, the looping process terminates, ensuring that all records have been successfully retrieved and processed.

Page 22

From the TRACE logs below, its evident that looping process call "Initiate MS Dynamics Call" executed 23 times indicating 23 pages.

Full Run in TRACE

 

Conclusion

Implementing pagination in SAP CI for the Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter ensures efficient data retrieval while preventing performance bottlenecks. By leveraging looping mechanisms, we can seamlessly fetch and process large datasets in manageable batches. This approach not only optimizes API calls but also ensures smooth end-to-end data transfer. Stay tuned for the next part of this series, where we explore pagination for next adapters.

I hope this helps.

Cheers,

Punith Oswal

I wrote the SQL statement as follows.

DECLARE T_RETURN TABLE (

);

However, I am getting the following error.

sql syntax error: incorrect syntax near ",": line : line 2 col col 18 (at pos 60), Code: 257, SQL State: HY000

I didn't use a comma.
Any idea what could be causing this?

Any help would be welcome.
